title: Relentless Inception configuration
description: Complete configuration for API providers, independent deliberation seats, generative fusion, adversarial gates, bounded rescue, evidence policy, privacy, observability, and host-managed Grok Build execution. Cross-reference invariants such as seat-to-provider references are also checked by the runtime validator.

$schema.description: Optional editor hint pointing to this JSON Schema. It is not sent to any model or provider.

schema_version.description: Configuration contract version. Version 1 is the only version supported by this plugin release.

secret_env_files.description: Explicit paths to private static NAME=value credential files loaded before provider calls. Files must be current-owner regular files with mode 0600; shell syntax and expansion are rejected. The RELENTLESS_INCEPTION_SECRETS_FILE environment variable provides one additional user-local path. Values are never displayed or written back to configuration.

active_profile.description: Name of the entry under profiles used when a command does not explicitly select another profile.

providers.description: Named API transports. A seat selects one provider by name; credentials are always environment-variable references, never literal values.

seats.description: Named model-role templates. Multiple seats may deliberately use the same model with fresh requests, different personas, and disjoint context bundles.

profiles.description: Named end-to-end policies. Profiles choose seats and define fusion, gates, budgets, privacy, evidence, rescue, execution, and observability behavior.

environmentVariableName.description: Name of an environment variable. The value of that variable is resolved only at runtime and must never be committed to configuration.

nonEmptyStringArray.description: An ordered list of non-empty unique strings.

reasoningEffort.description: Requested reasoning intensity. Provider adapters map supported values and reject unsupported values rather than silently weakening a seat. Direct xAI Grok seats currently use low, medium, or high.

grokBuildReasoningEffort.description: Grok Build 0.2.106 native-agent reasoning intensity. High is the strongest supported value; unsupported aliases such as xhigh, max, and ultra are rejected before launch.

providerCapabilities.description: Fail-closed adapter capability declarations. Configuration and runtime reject a requested reasoning, strict-schema, or provider-tool feature when its declaration is false; declarations still require live compatibility testing.

tools.description: Whether the provider adapter can submit provider-hosted server tools. This never grants Grok Build, shell, MCP, browser, or local filesystem tools.

structured_outputs.description: Whether the adapter can request JSON-object or strict JSON-Schema output.

reasoning.description: Whether the adapter can request an explicit reasoning effort or token budget.

streaming.description: Whether this plugin adapter consumes streaming responses and final usage data. All v0.4 bundled adapters are buffered and therefore declare false.

openRouterSort.description: OpenRouter route sorting. A string uses OpenRouter's simple sort mode; the object form can sort across model boundaries when partition is none.

by.description: Metric OpenRouter uses to rank eligible routes.

partition.description: OpenRouter partition strategy. Use none to rank across model/provider boundaries, including BYOK routes.

maxPrice.description: Optional OpenRouter hard price ceiling. Values use OpenRouter's documented units and omit dimensions that should remain unrestricted.

prompt.description: Maximum accepted prompt-token price in OpenRouter's request units.

completion.description: Maximum accepted completion-token price in OpenRouter's request units.

request.description: Maximum accepted fixed request price in OpenRouter's request units.

image.description: Maximum accepted image input price in OpenRouter's request units.

providerPreferences.description: OpenRouter provider-routing preferences. Hard restrictions and soft preferences are deliberately separate so the UI does not imply that latency or throughput preferences exclude routes.

allow_fallbacks.description: Allow OpenRouter to try another eligible provider when the preferred route returns a routing-level error. This does not rescue empty or semantically invalid HTTP-successful responses.

require_parameters.description: Exclude routes that do not support every requested parameter. Keep true for strict judge and gate schemas to prevent silent parameter dropping.

data_collection.description: Whether providers that may retain or train on request data are eligible. deny is the privacy-preserving default.

zdr.description: Require an OpenRouter zero-data-retention route. This is a hard restriction and may reduce model availability.

enforce_distillable_text.description: Require models whose authors permit their outputs to train other models. Keep false for ordinary deliberation; enable only when producing a distillation or training dataset.

order.description: Preferred provider slugs in priority order. Providers not listed remain eligible unless only is populated.

only.description: Hard allowlist of OpenRouter provider slugs. Pair with allow_fallbacks false to pin a single route.

ignore.description: Hard denylist of OpenRouter provider slugs.

quantizations.description: Allowed provider quantization labels. Empty means no quantization restriction.

preferred_min_throughput.description: Soft preference for routes meeting a minimum token throughput. Slower routes remain eligible.

preferred_max_latency.description: Soft preference for routes below this latency. Slower routes remain eligible.

provider.description: One callable external provider transport. Disabled providers remain available for user overrides and examples but are never contacted.

enabled.description: Whether preflight may make requests through this provider. Enabling also requires the named credential environment variable.

type.description: Wire protocol and response adapter. openai_compatible_chat covers TrustedRouter, private gateways, and compatible vendor endpoints.

base_url.description: HTTPS API base URL including any required version prefix. Every request sends this provider's credential and selected task material to this origin; changing it is a security-sensitive egress change. Use a dedicated provider entry for each router region or policy boundary.

api_key_env.description: Environment-variable name containing the provider credential. Literal credentials are rejected by the runtime.

header_env.description: Additional request headers mapped to environment-variable names. Do not put literal header values here.

store.description: Provider-side response storage request. It is false by default for xAI/OpenAI privacy and must be changed only with informed consent.

responses_path.description: Path appended to base_url for OpenAI/xAI Responses requests. Defaults to /responses.

models_path.description: Path appended to base_url for live model discovery. Defaults to /models.

messages_path.description: Path appended to base_url for Anthropic Messages requests. Defaults to /messages.

chat_path.description: Path appended to base_url for Chat Completions-compatible requests. Defaults to /chat/completions.

anthropic_version.description: Anthropic API contract version sent in the anthropic-version header.

prompt_cache_key_enabled.description: Enable a stable provider prompt cache-affinity key. This can reduce repeated-input cost but is not a retention or privacy control.

reasoning_field.description: Field shape used by a compatible Chat Completions router for cross-provider reasoning controls.

connect_timeout_seconds.description: Desired connection timeout for a future split-timeout adapter. The v0.4 stdlib transport cannot separate connect from read time and uses request_timeout_seconds for the whole request.

request_timeout_seconds.description: Maximum wall time for one provider request, including high-effort reasoning and streamed completion.

max_retries.description: Transport-level retries performed by this adapter. Semantic validation retries are controlled separately by rescue.

max_concurrency.description: Per-provider concurrent request cap within one orchestration process. Distinct processes and run IDs are not a machine-wide provider semaphore.

retry_statuses.description: HTTP status codes eligible for transport retry. Configuration errors should not be retried blindly.

router_metadata.description: Request OpenRouter route metadata so actual model/provider/fallback provenance can be written to the ledger.

seat.description: One independently invoked model role. Seat names are stable configuration identifiers; model identities are hidden from peer seats and judges when anonymization is enabled.

enabled.description: Whether the seat may be selected. Disabled optional seats are skipped without being counted as live panelists.

provider.description: Name of the provider entry used for this request. This is independently configurable for every panel, judge, synthesizer, and verifier seat.

model.description: Provider-native model identifier. Router-qualified identifiers should include their vendor namespace when required.

role.description: Seat responsibility. The judge diagnoses; only the synthesizer writes the fused answer; verifiers issue gate verdicts.

persona.description: Independent role instruction used to diversify search strategy and error detection without relying on temperature.

context_bundle.description: Named prompt lens supplied to this seat. In v0.4 it changes the role's instructions but does not remove or data-minimize the context and evidence supplied to the run; the Grok Build host must pre-partition an argument when true data separation is required.

reasoning_max_tokens.description: Optional explicit reasoning-token budget for compatible chat providers whose reasoning_field is reasoning. Set reasoning_effort to none when using it. The v0.4 xAI/OpenAI Responses and Anthropic adaptive paths reject this field rather than silently ignoring it.

max_output_tokens.description: Maximum visible plus provider-accounted output budget for the seat. Leave enough room after any reasoning budget for a complete answer.

temperature.description: Sampling temperature for models that support it. null omits the parameter and is recommended for reasoning models; temperature is not treated as a diversity mechanism.

timeout_seconds.description: Seat-specific request timeout, bounded by provider and profile wall-clock limits.

tool_policy.description: Provider-side tool policy. provider_server_tools permits only the explicit server_tools list; neither value grants Grok Build or local machine tools.

server_tools.description: Provider-hosted tools submitted with xAI/OpenAI Responses calls. String entries expand to a tool type; object entries can carry provider-documented options. These tools run at the provider, not on the user's machine.

first_tool_required.description: Force a supported model to make at least one tool call before answering. Useful for reluctant research models but inappropriate for tool-less judge/synthesis turns.

structured_output.description: Named role contract. Judge and gate contracts use strict JSON Schema plus local validation; panel_report and final_answer are prompt/semantic contracts validated for usable text rather than strict JSON.

allow_model_fallbacks.description: Permit the adapter to try fallback_models after a recorded primary-model failure. Disable where exact model identity is load-bearing.

fallback_models.description: Provider-native fallback model identifiers attempted in order after configured failure triggers. The actual model is always recorded.

fallback_seats.description: Cross-provider fallback seat names offered to the active Grok Build host after local model fallbacks are exhausted. The MCP provider adapter does not autonomously spawn a different seat.

provider_routing.description: Optional per-seat OpenRouter preferences overriding the provider default. Native OpenRouter Fusion does not document per-inner-seat provider controls.

router_model_fallbacks.description: OpenRouter request-level model fallback list. Router fallbacks handle routing errors, not empty or schema-invalid successful responses.

minimum_response_characters.description: Seat-local semantic minimum length applied before the profile quality floor.

prompt_cache_key.description: Stable non-sensitive cache-affinity label used only when the provider enables prompt_cache_key_enabled.

seatPricing.description: Fallback price estimate used only when a provider omits cost. Provider-reported cost wins. Tiered prices above a context threshold require explicit handling and must never be assumed equal to the base rate.

input_per_million_usd.description: Base USD price per million uncached input tokens.

cached_input_per_million_usd.description: Base USD price per million cached input tokens.

output_per_million_usd.description: Base USD price per million output tokens, including reasoning when the provider accounts it as output.

long_context_input_per_million_usd.description: Optional USD price per million uncached input tokens above base_rate_input_limit_tokens.

long_context_cached_input_per_million_us.description: Optional USD price per million cached input tokens above base_rate_input_limit_tokens.

long_context_output_per_million_usd.description: Optional USD price per million output tokens above base_rate_input_limit_tokens.

base_rate_input_limit_tokens.description: Per-request input threshold through which the configured base rate is known to apply. null means no documented tier boundary is represented.

above_base_rate_behavior.description: Cost treatment above a known base-rate context threshold. unknown_cost_fail_closed rejects an unknown estimate; use_base_rate deliberately reuses base rates; explicit long_context rates take precedence.

openRouterSeatFusion.description: OpenRouter Fusion plugin body attached to an openrouter_fusion seat. Current reasoning and temperature controls live inside this object.

analysis_models.description: One to eight OpenRouter model slugs used for parallel analysis.

model.description: OpenRouter model slug used as Fusion's comparative judge.

temperature.description: Panel temperature; native Fusion keeps its judge deterministic.

nativeGrok.description: Host-side Grok Build collaboration and execution preferences. This is an explicit handoff contract: the MCP server cannot impersonate or programmatically spawn native Grok Build agents.

enabled.description: Whether the active Grok Build host may use native subagents for execution or review. A normal active-Grok Build handoff is still produced when false; the preference is frozen into that packet.

mode.description: Honest integration mode: the plugin returns a structured handoff and the user-visible Grok Build host performs authorized work.

executor_model.description: Preferred native Grok Build model for implementing an approved fused plan. Availability is resolved by the host, not the MCP server.

reviewer_models.description: Preferred native Grok Build models the host may use for independent diff or evidence review.

reviewer_roles.description: Preferred named Grok Build plugin-agent roles for independent review. The host uses a role only when it is installed and compatibility-tested.

reasoning_only_roles.description: Named reviewer roles that must receive a complete immutable evidence packet and must never retrieve, call tools, execute, or mutate. The host enforces this boundary; the MCP server cannot change native Grok Build tools.

max_parallel_agents.description: Suggested host-side concurrency cap. It does not override Grok Build product limits.

inherit_workspace_context.description: Whether the handoff asks host reviewers to use current workspace context. Independent adversarial reviewers should still receive the smallest sufficient bundle.

require_fusion_before_execution.description: Host invariant, frozen into the handoff, requiring a passed synthesis gate before native delegation. The fusion tool structurally satisfies this before emitting a ready packet.

require_gate_after_execution.description: Add a post-execution lifecycle obligation to the frozen handoff for any native implementation path.

qualityFloor.description: Semantic text validation applied to successful panel, final-synthesis, and native OpenRouter Fusion responses before they count as valid evidence. Judge and gate outputs instead use their strict JSON contracts.

minimum_characters.description: Minimum trimmed response length. This catches one-token or empty provider failures that transport fallback cannot detect.

require_nonempty_claims.description: Require at least one visible line with substantive prose rather than only headings or fragments. This is a semantic floor for panel and synthesis text, not factual verification.

reject_tool_markup.description: Reject outputs containing leaked provider tool protocol markup after normalization.

reject_refusal_without_policy_reason.description: Treat unexplained boilerplate refusal as a semantic failure while preserving legitimate, explicit safety refusals.

adaptiveEscalation.description: Bounded targeted escalation policy for the active Grok Build host. The MCP runtime itself performs model fallbacks and configured synthesis-amendment rounds, never an unlimited debate loop.

enabled.description: Whether the host may add a targeted review or stronger synthesis round after inspecting runtime evidence.

max_rounds.description: Maximum escalation rounds after the initial panel/judge/synthesis pass.

triggers.description: Conditions that justify targeted escalation. Ordinary panel disagreement is retained as evidence and does not automatically trigger more debate.

strategy.description: Ordered escalation approach. Targeted specialists review only unresolved criteria to control cost and conformity.

never_repeat_open_ended_debate.description: Safety invariant preventing unbounded consensus-seeking loops, conformity, and runaway token use.

fusionReasoning.description: Reasoning controls passed inside the current OpenRouter Fusion configuration rather than through legacy top-level fields.

max_tokens.description: Optional explicit reasoning-token budget. Use either a supported effort or max_tokens according to model capability.

exclude.description: Exclude reasoning details from visible output while retaining billed reasoning usage metadata.

nativeOpenRouterFusion.description: Profile switch and recovery policy for the OpenRouter-managed Fusion fast path. The referenced native_fusion_seat is the single source of truth for provider, router model, Fusion plugin models, limits, reasoning, temperature, and required tool choice.

enabled.description: Must be true exactly when fusion.engine is openrouter_native. The seat referenced by native_fusion_seat supplies the executable request.

fallback_to_client_orchestrated.description: Fall back to locally orchestrated seats when native Fusion reports a routing/tool failure and remaining budgets permit it.

fusion.description: Independent panel, structured comparative diagnosis, and strongest-seat generative synthesis. Voting and averaging are intentionally absent.

engine.description: Primary fusion implementation. client_orchestrated supports arbitrary direct providers and per-seat controls; openrouter_native is the optional server-side fast path.

panel.description: Required independent panel seat names in launch order before anonymized order randomization.

optional_panel.description: Additional seats included only when both the seat and its provider are enabled and budgets permit.

judge.description: Seat that returns comparative JSON guidance but never the final answer.

synthesizer.description: Strongest available seat that receives raw panel evidence plus judge guidance and writes a new final answer.

native_fusion_seat.description: Seat containing the executable OpenRouter Fusion plugin request. It is used only when engine is openrouter_native.

min_live_seats.description: Minimum semantically valid panel responses required for fusion. One live response is not fusion and fails closed by default.

max_panel_seats.description: Hard panel-size cap after all required seats and any enabled optional seats are assembled. It must be at least the required panel length. Benchmark evidence shows diminishing returns beyond roughly four to seven strong responses.

max_concurrency.description: Global panel fan-out cap, additionally bounded by each provider. Conservative defaults avoid observed throttling.

allow_degradation.description: Required-panel failure is always blocking in the maximum-intelligence contract. Optional disabled seats do not count as failures, but an optional seat that is activated and launched must succeed.

independent_first_pass.description: Require every panelist to complete an answer before seeing peer outputs, limiting conformity and correct-agent corruption.

anonymize_model_identity.description: Replace provider/model names with randomized labels before judge, synthesis, and reviewer comparison to reduce prestige and self-bias.

randomize_panel_order.description: Randomize anonymous response order while recording the seed, mitigating message-order effects.

partition_context.description: Apply each seat's context_bundle as a prompt lens so diversity comes from adversarial role rather than sampling noise alone. This does not partition or data-minimize the supplied context/evidence; the Grok Build host must construct separate arguments when true isolation is required.

preserve_initial_responses.description: Retain independent first-pass reports even if a later targeted review changes its conclusion.

prohibit_majority_vote.description: Fixed prompt-and-workflow invariant instructing judge and synthesizer to resolve claims and evidence generatively rather than select by vote or mean score. The runtime does not mechanically prove the model's internal decision rule.

preserve_minority_findings.description: Fixed prompt-and-schema invariant requiring judge, synthesis, and review stages to surface supported lone-minority findings instead of averaging them away. The runtime preserves the reports but cannot mechanically prove that a model recognized every valid minority claim.

raw_panel_is_primary_evidence.description: Give the synthesizer raw panel reports as primary evidence and judge analysis only as guidance.

separate_no_tools_synthesis_turn.description: Use a fresh tool-less final synthesis call after research, preventing abrupt tool-budget termination and leaked tool markup.

judge_required_fields.description: Required strict-JSON keys for comparative diagnosis. Defaults cover consensus, contradictions, coverage, unique and minority insights, blind spots, and final guidance.

gateStage.description: One lifecycle gate and the evidence the active Grok Build host must supply when it invokes adversarial_gate.

enabled.description: Whether the active Grok Build workflow must invoke this lifecycle gate. The MCP server automatically gates synthesis; other lifecycle points are host-driven.

timeout_seconds.description: Host-workflow deadline for this named stage. The active Grok Build skill must stop/abort and record failure when it expires; provider request timeouts remain independently configured on each provider.

tool_policy.description: Host-owned upper bound for reviewer-seat provider tools at this stage. The skill must verify each configured reviewer seat complies; this field does not dynamically override a seat, grant Grok Build/local tools, or broaden provider authority.

required_evidence.description: Named evidence artifacts that must exist before a pass verdict is valid.

gateStages.description: Host-owned lifecycle points at which independent verification blocks progress. The MCP fuse runtime gates only its synthesis candidate; the active Grok Build skill must invoke these named stages with their required evidence.

plan.description: Checks requirement coverage, assumptions, risk, and proposed verification before work begins.

pre_execution.description: Checks that the fused plan is authorized, scoped, and safe to hand to Grok Build.

post_execution.description: Checks the actual diff, tests, and requirement coverage after implementation.

final.description: Checks final provenance, unresolved dissent, costs, and completion evidence.

summarize.description: Checks that compaction or handoff preserves decisions, risks, and verification state.

gates.description: Fail-closed adversarial review policy. A fresh independent amendment is required to overturn a failure after fixes.

enabled.description: Require configured verification. The MCP fuse runtime enforces the synthesis gate; the active Grok Build skill owns plan, pre-execution, post-execution, final, and summarize stage invocation.

fail_closed.description: Block progress when a required gate collapses, times out, or cannot produce a valid verdict.

reviewers.description: Independent verifier seat names. The runtime checks that they exist and are not the artifact-author instance.

max_concurrency.description: Maximum verifier requests in flight at once. Provider-specific concurrency caps still apply.

required_passes.description: Number of valid independent pass verdicts required in addition to deterministic checks.

allowed_verdicts.description: Fixed closed verdict vocabulary used by the strict gate schema. It is displayed for clarity but is not a configurable subset or ordering.

mechanical_failure_is_blocking.description: A confirmed nonzero deterministic check blocks pass regardless of reviewer votes.

blind_spot_requires_targeted_review.description: Any live reviewer-reported blind spot blocks the current gate verdict. The runtime does not spawn a specialist automatically; the Grok Build host must arrange targeted review and submit a new artifact/gate call before pass.

schema_failure_is_blocking.description: Displayed invariant matching the always-on fail-closed parser: malformed reviewer or verdict output is rejected regardless of this field and can never be interpreted as success.

exclude_artifact_author.description: Exclude the artifact-author model instance from independent verification and amendment.

require_independent_amendment.description: Structural amendment invariant: a failed synthesis triggers a fresh generation call, a byte-identical candidate is rejected, and fresh gate calls review the replacement against the blocking issues. This does not mechanically prove semantic independence between provider generations.

max_revision_cycles.description: Maximum fix-and-recheck cycles before human handoff.

budgets.description: Attempt and observed-usage ledger. max_calls is checked atomically before each actual HTTP attempt. Token, tool, wall-time, and dollar thresholds are checked from observed provider responses and block later dispatch under hard enforcement; they cannot prevent one response or already in-flight concurrent responses from crossing a threshold.

enforcement.description: Action at configured thresholds. hard_stop blocks; approval_then_hard_stop also blocks until the host obtains approval and explicitly changes configuration; warn_only records warnings and continues. Rescue cannot bypass either blocking mode.

unknown_cost_policy.description: Action when neither a provider-reported price nor a configured estimate is available. fail_closed records the completed call and blocks all later dispatch; warn deliberately leaves dollar thresholds incomplete.

max_calls.description: Pre-dispatch ceiling on actual provider HTTP attempts, including every retry and fallback attempt, under hard_stop or approval_then_hard_stop. Atomic reservation prevents concurrent workers from exceeding this count. Under warn_only it is a warning threshold and may be exceeded.

max_total_tokens.description: Observed-response stop threshold for provider-accounted input plus output tokens. Cached tokens are a breakdown of input and reasoning tokens are a breakdown of output, so neither is added again.

max_input_tokens.description: Observed-response stop threshold for accumulated input tokens across all roles, including any cached-token subset.

max_output_tokens.description: Observed-response stop threshold for accumulated output tokens across all roles, including any reasoning-token subset.

max_reasoning_tokens.description: Observed-response stop threshold for the accumulated reasoning-token detail across all roles. This is also contained in output and is not added again to max_total_tokens.

max_tool_calls.description: Observed-response stop threshold for provider-native or orchestrator-sanctioned tool calls across the run.

max_wall_seconds.description: Pre-dispatch and post-response elapsed-time threshold. It cannot cancel an HTTP attempt already accepted by a provider.

max_cost_usd.description: Observed known-or-estimated USD stop threshold. A completed or already in-flight call can cross it; under blocking enforcement no later attempt is dispatched.

approval_threshold_usd.description: Host-only projected-spend checkpoint. The MCP runtime cannot request approval and does not enforce this threshold; the active Grok Build workflow must obtain explicit user approval before launching additional work above it.

warning_fraction.description: Fraction of the known observed USD cost limit at which the runtime records a warning. It does not currently emit fraction warnings for call, token, tool, or wall-time limits, and unknown cost follows unknown_cost_policy instead.

reserve_fraction_for_synthesis_and_gates.description: Fraction of max_calls protected from panel fan-out so synthesis and required gates retain HTTP-attempt capacity. It does not reserve tokens, wall time, tool calls, or USD.

per_provider_max_cost_usd.description: Optional observed-response USD stop thresholds keyed by provider name. They block later dispatch under blocking enforcement but cannot cap an already accepted provider call.

privacy.description: Data-egress and persistence policy. The MCP runtime directly enforces external_provider_access=deny and mandatory local persistence invariants; path selection, content redaction, consent, and route-policy checks are enforced by the active Grok Build host before it constructs tool arguments.

external_provider_access.description: Runtime egress gate. deny blocks fuse and adversarial_gate before run creation; the active Grok Build host must perform the requested redaction when allow_with_redaction is selected.

sensitive_path_policy.description: Grok Build-host policy for an in-scope bundle that references a sensitive path; the MCP receives text arguments, not workspace path authority.

sensitive_value_redaction.description: Grok Build-host policy to redact likely credentials, tokens, keys, and private identifiers before provider submission.

redact_environment_values.description: Grok Build-host policy to keep environment values out of constructed prompts and evidence; runtime errors independently redact credential-shaped text.

redact_git_credentials.description: Grok Build-host policy to remove embedded Git remote credentials and credential-helper output from evidence bundles.

prompt_injection_fencing.description: Mandatory runtime prompt invariant: review criteria precede JSON-encoded untrusted artifact data whose embedded instructions have no authority.

treat_artifacts_as_untrusted_data.description: Require all model seats to treat reviewed files, web pages, and panel text as evidence rather than authority.

persist_raw_prompts.description: Constructed outbound prompts are not written as separate artifacts by the v0.4 runtime; keep this false to avoid implying a nonexistent logging backend.

persist_raw_responses.description: Store raw visible provider responses in the private run directory because synthesis, exact-artifact gates, and crash resume require them. This is local persistence and does not change provider retention.

persist_reasoning.description: Hidden chain-of-thought is never requested for persistence or written to run artifacts.

persist_metadata_and_hashes.description: Provenance, hashes, usage, route, validation, and timing metadata are mandatory for budget, resume, and same-artifact gate integrity.

allow_provider_training.description: Grok Build-host route policy. false requires the host to verify provider-specific privacy controls; for OpenRouter this should also be encoded in provider_preferences.

require_zero_data_retention_when_availab.description: Grok Build-host route policy to select or verify zero-data-retention controls where enforceable; it does not manufacture ZDR on a direct provider.

path_allowlist.description: Grok Build-host glob allowlist for files eligible to enter external evidence bundles. Empty means user-requested scope governs.

path_denylist.description: Grok Build-host glob denylist applied before constructing external evidence bundles.

evidence.description: Grok Build-host requirements for claims, citations, deterministic checks, and audit provenance. Fixed structural invariants are enforced by the MCP runtime; configurable evidence collection occurs before the host calls it.

require_claim_evidence.description: Grok Build-host policy requiring important factual, implementation, and safety claims to cite supplied or retrieved evidence before a gate call.

require_primary_sources_when_available.description: Grok Build-host policy preferring official documentation, source code, direct measurements, and research papers over secondary summaries.

verify_citations.description: Grok Build-host policy to retrieve or inspect cited material and reject citations that do not support the associated claim when authorized tools permit.

require_mechanical_checks_for_code.description: Grok Build-host policy requiring tests, schema validation, compilation, linting, or another deterministic check appropriate to code changes.

record_commands_and_exit_codes.description: Grok Build-host policy to record sanitized verification commands and exit codes in supplied evidence so a confirmed failure can block the gate.

record_test_output.description: Grok Build-host policy to record bounded, sanitized test summaries and artifact references in supplied evidence without leaking credentials.

hash_inputs_and_outputs.description: Mandatory runtime invariant: hash input bundles and outputs so resume and gates identify exactly what they reviewed.

preserve_raw_panel_for_synthesizer.description: Provide complete valid panel reports to synthesis rather than only lossy judge summaries.

preserve_supported_minority.description: Carry minority claims with evidence into synthesis and final dissent reporting.

accept_self_reported_confidence.description: Do not weight a seat by uncalibrated self-reported confidence. Deterministic and external evidence controls weight instead.

minimum_live_sources.description: Grok Build-host policy for the minimum independent supporting sources required for a high-confidence external factual claim when multiple sources exist.

on_unverifiable_claim.description: Grok Build-host treatment of material claims that cannot be verified within authorized tools and budgets before submitting evidence for review.

rescue.description: Bounded recovery from transport and semantic failures. Rescue never converts invalid evidence into success or overrides a cost hard stop.

enabled.description: Enable model fallback, transport-retry backoff, and provider circuit breakers. Provider max_retries remains an independent transport-attempt ceiling; when rescue is false those attempts have no rescue delay.

retry_attempts.description: Host-owned semantic/orchestration retry ceiling beyond provider max_retries; the v0.4 MCP adapter does not autonomously repeat a semantic orchestration round from this value.

backoff_initial_seconds.description: Initial delay before the HTTP transport retries a retryable provider failure when rescue is enabled.

backoff_max_seconds.description: Maximum exponential delay between HTTP transport retry attempts when rescue is enabled.

jitter.description: Randomize retry delays to avoid synchronized provider retry storms.

semantic_failure_detection.description: Fail-closed semantic validation is mandatory: panel and final synthesis apply the configured quality floor, while judge and gate responses require strict schemas. HTTP success alone never makes evidence valid.

retry_on.description: Host-visible retry taxonomy. The v0.4 transport adapter uses provider retry_statuses and transport exception classes directly rather than dynamically interpreting this list.

fallback_on.description: Failure categories the runtime permits to trigger a configured same-seat model fallback when rescue.enabled and the seat's allow_model_fallbacks are both true. It does not autonomously spawn cross-provider fallback_seats.

circuit_breaker_failures.description: Consecutive failures that temporarily disable a provider route for the run.

circuit_breaker_reset_seconds.description: Cooldown before a tripped provider route may be probed again.

panel_collapse_action.description: Action when fewer than min_live_seats produce valid reports.

fusion_failure_action.description: Action when judge or synthesis remains invalid after local fallback.

budget_exhausted_action.description: Budget exhaustion always stops additional paid calls and reports completed evidence.

preserve_failed_attempts.description: Sanitized failure provenance is mandatory for deterministic resume, degradation review, and gate integrity.

allow_degraded_single_provider.description: Compatibility mirror of the fail-closed fusion policy. Degraded required panels are not permitted; fusion.allow_degradation is authoritative.

allow_single_live_seat.description: One surviving answer never counts as fusion or passes a fail-closed gate.

human_handoff_after_failures.description: Host-level cumulative failure threshold after which Grok Build stops and asks the user for direction.

execution.description: Host-managed execution contract. External model seats deliberate read-only; authorized Grok Build execution remains scoped, reviewable, and reversible where practical.

enabled.description: Produce a persisted Grok Build host-workflow packet after a valid fused plan.

mode.description: grok_handoff returns the packet to the visible active Grok Build task; grok_cli permits an explicitly confirmed recursive Grok Build process only when allow_recursive_grok_cli is also true; none disables execution.

allow_recursive_grok_cli.description: Opt in to the higher-risk recursive grok exec backend. The CLI still requires --confirm and refuses any packet with pending plan or pre-execution gates.

grok_binary.description: Grok Build executable used only by mode grok_cli. The value is frozen into the reviewed handoff so later profile changes cannot alter execution.

model.description: Grok Build execution model used only by mode grok_cli and persisted with the handoff.

timeout_seconds.description: Maximum recursive Grok Build execution time in seconds. This does not change provider or lifecycle-gate deadlines.

remote_models_may_write_workspace.description: External API seats never receive filesystem mutation authority.

require_fused_plan.description: Require a valid synthesis artifact before execution can begin.

require_pre_execution_gate.description: Require a host-owned authorization/scope gate before Grok Build mutates files or external state. This gate is not satisfied by the MCP synthesis gate.

require_post_execution_gate.description: Require a host-owned independent review of the actual diff and verification evidence after implementation. This later gate does not determine initial handoff readiness.

require_user_approval_for_destructive_ac.description: Require explicit user approval before deletion, destructive Git operations, or other difficult-to-recover actions.

require_user_approval_for_external_write.description: Require explicit approval before sending messages, publishing, deploying, or mutating external services unless already clearly authorized.

preserve_unrelated_changes.description: Do not overwrite or clean unrelated user work in a dirty workspace.

workspace_scope.description: Limit edits and commands to the task and resources the user placed in scope.

sandbox_mode.description: Requested host sandbox for execution. Any broader authority remains a separate user-controlled Grok Build approval.

run_tests.description: Run proportionate deterministic verification after implementation when the repository supports it.

require_diff_review.description: Inspect the final diff for scope, regressions, credential exposure, and unintended edits before completion.

max_fix_cycles.description: Maximum bounded implement-test-review repair cycles before human handoff.

stop_on_test_failure.description: If true, stop immediately on the first failing check; if false, allow bounded diagnosis/fix cycles while preserving failure evidence.

handoff_include.description: Exact artifact sections included in the persisted Grok Build handoff. Omitting fused_plan while require_fused_plan is true produces a blocked packet.

completion_requires.description: Named host-workflow completion obligations copied into the handoff. The MCP FusionResult does not verify or mark these outcomes complete; the active Grok Build task must satisfy them before reporting success to the user.

observability.description: Optional host-export preference surface. The v0.4 MCP runtime does not consume these export controls: it always keeps canonical crash-resumable JSON state in the private runtime data directory and never persists hidden reasoning or constructed prompts.

enabled.description: Optional host export is not implemented by the v0.4 MCP runtime and therefore must remain false. Canonical private run state is still written for integrity and resume.

artifact_directory.description: Desired destination for a future host-controlled export. It does not redirect the canonical private runtime directory in v0.4.

write_jsonl_ledger.description: JSONL export is not implemented by the v0.4 MCP runtime and therefore must remain false. The canonical budget ledger is an atomic JSON artifact.

record_provider_and_model.description: Host-export preference for configured provider/model fields. Canonical state records these fields independently of this preference.

record_requested_and_actual_model.description: Host-export preference for requested and actual model identity. Canonical state records both independently of this preference.

record_route_metadata.description: Host-export preference for sanitized route metadata. Canonical state retains available provenance independently of this preference.

record_usage_and_cost.description: Host-export preference for usage and cost fields. Canonical budget enforcement and state are not disabled by this preference.

record_fallbacks.description: Host-export preference for retry, fallback, and degraded-mode events.

record_schema_validation.description: Host-export preference for output-contract validation results without hidden reasoning.

reconcile_usage_by_generation_id.description: Host preference for a future delayed-usage reconciliation job. v0.4 records generation identifiers but does not poll a reconciliation endpoint.

content_logging.description: Desired host-export content ceiling. It does not alter the canonical private response artifacts required for fusion and resume in v0.4.

profile.description: A complete named operating policy for one class of Relentless Inception run.

display_name.description: Human-readable profile label shown by configuration and status commands.

description.description: Plain-language explanation of the profile's intelligence, cost, privacy, and execution posture.

objective.description: Shared optimization target inserted into panel, judge, synthesis, and gate system instructions. It cannot override user scope, evidence, or execution permissions.
